mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains below 0.5% while CPI inflation accelerates above 7%, creating a negative real rate environment that incentivizes equity allocation as inflation hedge. This persistent real rate compression drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as portfolio managers rotate into equities to preserve purchasing power.
evidence_refs: fact:As of Feb 2022, FEDFUNDS=0.39% and CPIAUCSL=239.927 (YoY ~7.9%)
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when trigger condition is met, or Rank IC between real rate proxy and forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate remains below 5% while payroll employment grows month-over-month, signaling a tight labor market that sustains consumer spending power and corporate earnings growth. This tight labor condition produces positive equity returns as the market prices in sustained demand and profit margins.
evidence_refs: fact:As of Jan 2022, UNRATE=4.9% and PAYEMS=144448.0 thousand
regime_hint: tight labor market with strong employment growth
failure_condition: Correlation between UNRATE and 20-day forward returns becomes positive (> 0.1) over any 12-month rolling window, or SPX produces negative returns in more than 50% of months when UNRATE < 5% and PAYEMS growth is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measured by HOUST decline by more than 10% month-over-month, signaling a demand shock in the construction and real estate sectors that propagates to broader economic weakness. This leading indicator of demand destruction produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward-looking equity markets price in reduced economic activity.
evidence_refs: fact:As of Dec 2021, HOUST=1189.0 thousand units
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances following a >10% HOUST decline, or Rank IC between HOUST momentum and forward returns is below 0.01 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index INDPRO declines month-over-month while real sales RSAFS remain flat or decline, indicating a supply-side contraction coupled with demand weakness. This stagflationary signal produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices earnings expectations downward and increases equity risk premium.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < 0% AND RSAFS month-over-month change < 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:As of Dec 2021, INDPRO=104.1342 and RSAFS=457727.0 million dollars
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when both INDPRO and RSAFS decline, or Rank IC of the interaction term falls below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales RSAFS accelerate with month-over-month growth exceeding 1%, indicating robust consumer demand that supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations. This demand strength produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices earnings expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:As of Jan 2022, RSAFS=457727.0 million dollars
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when RSAFS MoM growth exceeds 1%, or Rank IC between RSAFS growth and forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: CPI inflation measured by CPIAUCSL accelerates to levels above 7% year-over-year while the Federal Reserve maintains FEDFUNDS below 1%, creating policy lag that the market interprets as future rate hikes. Anticipation of tightening produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as equity valuations compress due to higher discount rates.
evidence_refs: fact:As of Feb 2022, CPIAUCSL=239.927 (YoY ~7.9%) and FEDFUNDS=0.39%
regime_hint: monetary policy lag with accelerating inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when inflation exceeds 7% and rates remain below 1%, or Rank IC of the policy lag interaction falls below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, signaling labor market deterioration that precedes broader economic weakness. This deterioration produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices recession probability and corporate earnings expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances following a >0.3pp UNRATE rise over 3 months, or Rank IC between UNRATE changes and forward returns is below 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 remains positive with quarter-over-quarter growth above 0.5% annualized, while housing starts HOUST remain above 1100 thousand units, indicating sustained economic expansion and construction demand. This dual confirmation of demand strength produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market prices in continued earnings growth.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth > 0.5% AND HOUST > 1100 thousand
evidence_refs: fact:As of Q4 2021, GDPC1=16575.1 billion chained 2017 dollars; as of Dec 2021, HOUST=1189.0 thousand units
regime_hint: sustained economic expansion with strong construction activity
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when both GDPC1 growth exceeds 0.5% annualized and HOUST exceeds 1100 thousand, or Rank IC of the expansion confirmation interaction falls below 0.03 over any 24-month rolling window.
